question.label: Arrival context
question.name: What's been most on your mind at work lately?
question.description: Gives Elena a low-pressure opening to set context in her own words before anything specific is raised.

answer: I do not think anything is broken, but I do feel less grounded than I did before.

assessment.note: She says nothing is broken but feels less grounded than before, which signals mild strain and reduced clarity; also names a concrete thread in 'less grounded'.

new_queue.label: Less grounded
new_queue.name: When you say 'less grounded' — what feels less steady day to day?

new_queue.label: Load pressure
new_queue.name: What part of your current load is hardest to keep in balance?

new_queue.label: Support gap
new_queue.name: Where would a bit more support make the biggest difference right now?

new_queue.label: Next step
new_queue.name: What's the first concrete thing you want to have changed by our next conversation?

issues: coverage: injected growth into next question (0 touches after turn 4)

history.answer_excerpt: I can feel the shift since the reorg, even though I am trying to keep things stable for the team.
I have been quieter in leadership meetings because I am still figuring out where my voice has most leverage.
I do not think anything is broken, but I do feel less grounded than I did before.
I can feel the shift since the reorg, even though I am trying to keep things stable for the team.
I have been quieter in leadership meetings because I am still figuring out where my voice has most leverage.
Part of me is tired from constant context switching, and I have not really said that out loud.
I do not think anything is broken, but I do feel less grounded than I did before.
